channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Businesses experiment with cheaper Chinese AI models as U.S. rivals become pricier
item.description: Lower costs and open-source availability are leading some companies to test Chinese AI models, despite ongoing security concerns.

item.title: Jensen Huang's signature black leather jacket sells at Sotheby's for nearly $1 million, benefiting young tech builders
item.description: The Nvidia CEO's Tom Ford jacket, photomatched to a 2023 Taipei appearance, was estimated at $40,000-$60,000.

item.title: OpenAI's CFO: Four questions to determine if your AI spending is effective
item.description: Sarah Friar says CFOs should evaluate AI based on the work it completes, not just cost per token.

item.title: Markets may have just experienced their second DeepSeek shock, this time from a Chinese AI lab named after a Pink Floyd album
item.description: K3 could challenge the belief that U.S. firms can maintain their lead simply by outspending Chinese rivals on computing power.
